(3) It shall comc in to force at once- 2' Definition' - In these rules, unless there is any-thing repugnant in the subject or context: (a) "Act" means the Street vendors (Protections of Livelihood and Regulation of Street Vending) Act,20l4. rw6ur (b) "Appropriate Government" means the State Government in respect of matters relating to the State Govemment as referred under sub section (1) of section 2 of the Act; (c) "Holding capacity" means ihe maximum ndmber of street vendors who can be accommodated in any vending zone and has been determined as such by the local authority on the recommendations of the Tou,n \iending committee as referred undersub-section 2(lXb) of the Act. -- 1 of 13 -- "Local authoritv" means a I\4unicipal Ccrpor.rtiorl or a N4unicipal Council or a Nagar Panchayat, bY rvhater'er name callec!. or the Cantomrent Board. or as the case may be, a ciril area comtltiltee appointed under secrion -{7 ol the Cantonment Acl, 2006 or such other body entitled to function as a local authority in any city or to$'n to provide civic services and regulate street vending and includes the "planning authority,, which regulates the land use in that city or town; "Mobile vendor" means Street Vendors whc carry out.vending activities in designated area by moving from one place to another place as referred under subsection (2)(l)(d) of Section-2 of the Act. "Stationary vendors" lneans street vendors .r'ho carry out vending activities on regular basis at a specific location as referred under sub-section 2 (lXk) of the Act. "Natural market" means a market where sellers and buyers have traditionally congregated for the sale and purchase of products or services and has been determined as such by the local authority on the recommendations of the Town Vending Committee as referred under sub-section 2(l)(e) of the Act. 'Notification" means a notification published in the official gaz,ette as referred under sub. Section (2)(l)(f) of section-2 of the Act. "Planning arrthority" means a.Municipality or any Urban Development Authority or any other authority in any city or town as referred under Subsection 2(lXg) of section 2 of the Act. "Scheme" means a scheme framed by the appropriate Corernment under Section 38 of the Act. "strcet vendor" means a person engaged in vending as referred under sub-section 2(1XL) of Section -2 of the Act. "Schedule" means Schedule annexed to these rules. ' (m) "Section" rneans of Section of the Act. (n) "Town Vending Committee" means the body constituted by appropriate Government under section 22 of the Act. (o) "Vending zone" means an area or place or a location designated as such by the planning authority as referred under sub-Section 2(1)(n) of section -2 of the Act. The term ot, and the manner of constituting of Town vending committee' - (1) The Government shall constitute a Town Vending committee (TVC) in each local authority as referred under section 22(l),the :treet vendors (protection of livelihood and regulation of street vending) act'2014. Provided that the appropriate Government may, if considers necessary' provide for constitution of more than or. fo*" Vending Committee' or a Town Vending Committee for each zone or rvard, in each local authorrty. (5) The representatives from Non-Goverrunent Organizations/ Community Based Organizations and Street vending organizations/ Associations/ Federations will be nominated by the District Magistrate. The applications received for membership in each category will be shortlisted based on pre-defined eligibility criteria. If application received for a particular category is more than the required numbers, the District Magistrate and the Chairperson of the TVC will select the member on thp basis of lottery. Such lotter-v- should be held in the presence of the interested parties. -- 4 of 13 -- (e) The formation of the TVC including the nominated members wiil be publisheJ in the Government gazette b I the appropriate Goverrunent' (f) The details of the TVC rvill be published on the r'vebsite of the appropriate (7)Changeoftheseatingmemberaftertwoconsecutivemeetingswilibedesirable, however he/ she may be continued if there is no response to fresh advertisement and further the seating candidate has no objection to continue' In case of no response from any fresh candidate and unwillingness of the existing member to continue, the appropriate Government may nominate a suitable candidate of the group represented by the existing incumbent: Provided further the nomination of members representing informal/formal street vendors associations or federations shall be carried out on the basis of such criteria as may be taid down by the Goverunent' after inviting applications, as such, by publishing the same in at least two local newspapers or Publishing in anY other manncr' 4.1. Removal of a member of Town Vending Committee. - Any membqr of a Town Vending Committee may be removed by the Go-vernment from the committee' if he' (a) persistently makes defaults in the ierformance of his duties imposed on him under the Act and thise rules or exceeds or abuses its powers; (b) Remains absent for three consecutive meetings of the committee without the permission of the ChairPerson; ic) Is convicted of any crimin4l case by any court.of law: provided that such *.*U"r ,t att U" liven a reasonable opportunity of being heard before his removal' 5. The m,ti;e of the meeting will contain place, date and time of the meeting and a list of business will be transacted at the meeting; (3) chairperson rvill issue the notice before Seven days of the scheduled meeting; ug".riu of the items will be circulated to the members and put up on ofhcial designated rvebsite. Each agenda item will be also accompanied by a detailed notebringingouttheissuesinvolvedwithclearrecommendationbythe administration. The agenda papers including the notes should be'in Hindi or English, if the TVC decides so; ^ -- 5 of 13 -- 7. (4) Meeting of the committee will oe held at least once every three months: (5) Reqtrisition meeting can be onh' called by Chairperson on a specific issue on tlie. request of minimum l/3rd of the total mernbers of the TVC. The meeting rvill be called rvithin 15 days after receiving the reqLrest for the sanre from the required number of members; (6) The first meeting of TVC will be fixed within 15 days of the publication of the Gazette notification; (7) Decision will be taken based on the majority of present members in the nreeting- In :ase of equality of votes, the Chairperson will have the riglit of casting vote; (8) The Member Secretary will maintain a minute book for recording and proceedings of every meeting and rvill keep it in his safe custody. The minute ' book rvill record the signature of the members attending thc rneeting before its commencement and proper recording of proceedings and resolutions adopted at every meeting lvill be maintained in the minute's book. Minute's book rvould be put up in the subsequent meeting of TVC for confirrnation by the Chairperson; (9) Qu.rrum - The quorum for the meeting will be at least l/3rd of the total members of TVC- Meeting will not be carried out in the absence of the quorum and it will be adjoumed; (10) Following functions will'be discharged in the meeting of TVC :- (i) Final Decision about Vending Zones along with their holding capacity as worked out by the local authority; (ii) Issuing, withholding, suspending and cancelling of the Vending Certificate; (iii) Getting a social audit initiated on the implementation of the Act; (iv) For deciding the zoning, the TVC will have the base materials/ data fiom the local authority. Regulations and the planning w'ill identiff the Vendipg ' Zones. In case the TVC has got any points to be made about the zone or about the area to be allotted to each individual vendor it can drarv the attention of the planning and local authority to suitably modiff the plan. After obtaining the opinion of the authority, the TVC may take an appropriate decision; (v) On the recomrnendation of the local authority, TVC will declare the natural market, weekly market, heritage market, festive market, seasonal market, night bazaar and nicfe market with their exact location and specific period in case of seasonal nrarket or festival market; (vi) While making recommendations and suggesting changes about the Vending Zones, the TVC rvill have to take into consideration the roacl width, traffic flow and the pedestrian movement in the concerned area; The manner and the purpose for rvhich a person mav be associated rvith TVC.- Under Sub-section (1) of sectio'r 24 of the Act, TVC may associate any expert in the field of informal economy for Street Vending and Spatial Planning. Such an expert will have the right to take part in any meeting and give suggestions but he cannot cast his vote in the meeting.
